stories.headline: At least 17 people killed in Russia’s deadliest attack on Ukraine this year
stories.summary: More than 100 injured across country after Russia launches nearly 700 drones and dozens of ballistic and cruise missilesEurope live – latest updatesRussia has carried out its deadliest attack against Ukraine this year, killing at least 17 people and injuring more than 100 in a wave of drone and missile strikes across the country.Nine people died in the southern port city of Odesa and four were killed in Kyiv, including a 12-year-old boy. stories.headline: Israel agrees to 10-day Lebanon ceasefire

stories.summary: Trump had announced earlier on Thursday that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon would speak about halting the fighting, in what would have been the first direct conversation in more than 30 years.

stories.headline: Budget Airline Halts LA Flights as High Jet Fuel Costs Spill Into US Market
stories.summary: Norse Atlantic ASA, a Norwegian budget airline, has cut all of its flights to and from Los Angeles, citing high costs of jet fuel as disruptions from the war in Iran continue to tighten oil supplies and send prices soaring.

stories.headline: Is it worth stockpiling cash as phsyical currency makes comeback in Poland?
stories.summary: While the trend towards the complete digitalisation of payments prevailed not long ago, today central banks and governments say that physical money may prove indispensable in crisis situations.

stories.headline: See Pivot In Gold Trading: Suki Cooper
stories.summary: Suki Cooper, global head, commodities research at Standard Chartered Bank, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Markets." Gold has fallen about 9% since the start of the war, with a liquidity squeeze in the early weeks of fighting leading investors to offload holdings and cover losses elsewhere. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Netflix Earnings Put Focus Back on Basics as M&A Overhang Clears
stories.summary: Netflix Inc.’s stock struggled through the winter as the company engaged in a bidding war for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. But since ending the chase two months ago, the company has gotten back to basics, a strategy welcomed by investors and that’s expected to be validated when it reports earnings after the close.

stories.headline: Bondholders Agree Payment Pause for Crisis Hit Emerging Markets
stories.summary: Developing countries will be given extra time to meet debt payments when hit by natural or economic disasters under plans drawn up by the UK government and a group of international bondholders.

stories.headline: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom

stories.headline: Federal judge sets new limits on Trump ballroom construction
stories.summary: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon clarified that some national security work can continue while the overall project is halted.

stories.headline: ECB Should Keep Options Open Ahead of April Meeting, Nagel Says
stories.summary: It would be a mistake for the European Central Bank to signal where interest rates will be headed later this month, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.

stories.headline: Argentina in Talks for $2 Billion Loan Backed by World Bank
stories.summary: Argentina is negotiating a $2 billion loan with private banks backed by guarantees from World Bank institutions to pay down a relevant portion of its upcoming debt maturities.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
